text.title: Gcp Cloud Run Serverless Failure Modes
text.summary: 5 antipatterns commonly observed when applying the gcp-cloud-run-serverless methodology. Each pairs a symptom + root-cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Reads return inconsistent data across requests; users see stale or missing data.
antipattern.root-cause: Code persists state to local disk or in-process memory.
antipattern.fix: Move state to Cloud SQL / Firestore / Cloud Storage; redeploy.

antipattern.symptom: CPU-bound work throttled at default concurrency=80; single-threaded apps overloaded.
antipattern.root-cause: `gcloud run services describe` shows `concurrency: 80` for a CPU-heavy app.
antipattern.fix: Set explicit `--concurrency` matching app profile (1 for single-threaded, 10–30 for CPU-bound).

antipattern.symptom: Cold-start latency surprises users on quiet endpoints.
antipattern.fix: Document min-instances trade-off (cost vs cold start) in deploy manifest.

antipattern.symptom: Reproducibility lost; image swap can introduce regressions silently.
antipattern.root-cause: Service image deployed with floating tag (e.g. `:latest`).
antipattern.fix: Pin image by digest (`@sha256:...`); update digest via CI/CD only.

antipattern.symptom: Secrets embedded in image layer leak via registry access.
antipattern.fix: Mount via Secret Manager (`--set-secrets`); rotate any leaked secrets.
